entities.name: V
entities.context: Vrijen Attawar, the user whose knowledge system and training protocols are being documented

entities.name: Session Type Classification
entities.context: Framework for mapping workout descriptions to canonical session types: upper, lower, core, full_body, mobility

entities.name: Upper Circuit
entities.context: Upper-biased workout session type involving push-ups and rows

entities.name: Lower Circuit
entities.context: Lower-biased workout session type involving squats and bridges

entities.name: Core Session
entities.context: Core-focused workout session type targeting abs

entities.name: Full Body Session
entities.context: Mixed or complete workout covering multiple muscle groups

entities.name: Mobility Session
entities.context: Recovery-focused session involving stretching and foam rolling

entities.name: Signal Matching
entities.context: Process of interpreting V's natural language workout descriptions into canonical session types using keyword signals

relationships.context: V's workout logging system classifies each session into a canonical type
'upper' is one of the five canonical session types
'lower' is one of the five canonical session types
'core' is one of the five canonical session types
'full_body' is one of the five canonical session types
'mobility' is one of the five canonical session types
Signal matching is the mechanism that powers session type classification from natural language input
V provides natural language descriptions that are interpreted via signal matching into session types
